schemes.name: Indira Mahila Shakti Udyam Protsahan Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme launched by the Rajasthan government to empower women by providing them with loans and other financial assistance to start or expand their businesses
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant's age should be 18 years or more.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Rajasthan.
1. The women's self-help group or group of these groups (cluster/ federation) must be registered under any department of the state government and in case of cluster/federation of groups, it will be necessary to be registered under the Cooperative Act as per the rules.
schemes.benefits:
Loan Limit
- Loan amount of ₹ 50,00,000/- to individual women.
- Loan amount of Rs 1,00,00,000/- to self-help groups.
Margin Money
- A margin money of 25% of the sanctioned loan amount will be provided under the scheme. For women belonging to Scheduled Castes/Tribes, Scheduled Castes, widows, abandoned women, victims of violence and disabled women, the margin money will be 30% of the sanctioned loan amount.
- Note
:
- The maximum limit of margin money will be ₹ 15,00,000/-.
- The applicant's own contribution (5% / 10% of the project proposal) will be considered for margin money.
- Land value will not be included in the project proposal. The maximum limit of loan amount payable for construction of workshop/building will be up to 20% of the sanctioned amount of the project proposal.
- The maximum limit of loan for business will be Rs. 10 lakh. Business refers to wholesale or retail purchase-sale of commercial products.
Promotion of collateral security free loans:
- As per the guidelines of the Reserve Bank of India, collateral security will not be required for loans up to Rs. 10 lakh. Loans above Rs. 10 lakh can be linked with the Credit Guarantee Trust Fund for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE). The fee amount will be borne by the beneficiary. If the applicant voluntarily wants to give collateral security on the loan, they can do so.

schemes.description: The Labor department of Rajasthan started "Nirman Shramik Avm Unke Aashrit Bacchon Dwara Bhartiya Rajasthan Prashasnik Seva Hetu Aayojit Pratibhabhik Yojana" to help the children of construction workers.

- On passing the preliminary examination of Rajasthan Administrative Service – ₹50,000/-

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship
schemes.description: The scholarship is designed to provide OBC students with the necessary financial resources studying at pre-matriculation stage to enable them to complete their education.
schemes.eligibility:
1. Native and studying in Uttar Pradesh. 
1. Annual income of the parents/guardians of the students upto Rs. 2 Lakh.
1. Students studying in government schools, aided and recognized educational institutes (provided the availability of the budget). ।
schemes.benefits:
- Monthly Rs. 150/- for a maximum of 10 months.
- Annually Rs. 750/- ad hoc grant in one go.
schemes.application_process:
Online
- First of all, OBC students need to click on Option in the Student Section of  
website.
- Student Registration.
- Fill out the application form.
- Print out of application form.
- Deposit the print out along with all the enclosures in the educational institute.
- Verify and forward the application form by an educational institute.
- Verify and forward the application by Education Officer.
- Examine and match the data at state N.I.C. level.
- Approve/disapprove by the District Approval Committee.
- Lock the approved data with the digital signature by District Backward Class Welfare Officer.
- Generate the demand by the Directorate based on the data available on N.I.C.
- Depositing of the amount directly in the bank account of the student.

schemes.name: Shop Construction/Shop Operation Scheme For Rehabilitation Of PwDs
schemes.description: The "Shop Construction/Shop Operation Scheme For Rehabilitation Of PwDs" Scheme has been started by the Government of Uttar Pradesh and operated by the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Uttar Pradesh.
schemes.eligibility:
1. Certificate of minimum 40% disability (Certificate issued by Chief Medical Officer, Doctor of Community Health Centre/Primary Health Centre will only be valid).
   1. Persons with Disabilities of all categories who are citizens of Uttar Pradesh.
   1. The age of the Applicant should not be less than 18 years and should be less than 60 years.
   1. Persons with Disabilities who are not convicted in any criminal or financial matters and no government money/amount should be payable against them
   1. Persons with Disabilities should own 110 sq. ft. of land for shop construction or should be able to buy/take the land of the said area from their resources.
   1. The tenant should be given a tenure lease for a period of 5 years by the Persons with Disabilities for the operation of the shop available to them (Rent and working capital)
   1. Persons with disabilities whose annual income does not exceed twice the income limit prescribed by the government from time to time to the poverty line.

schemes.eligibility: 1. The player must be a bonafide native of Uttar Pradesh. 1. The sportspersons for at least three consecutive times for the year 2020-21 (year 2018-19, 2019-20 & 2020-21) must have been a member of the state senior team for the year 2021-22 (2019-20, 2020-21 & 2021-22) participated in the National Games/National Senior Championship and also the medal earned by him in the year for which the award is recommended. 1. If a player has participated in the National Senior Championship and he/she will participate in the international competitions mentioned in the government order such as the Olympic Games, World Championship/World Cup (held at an interval of 04 years) Asian Games, Commonwealth Games, Asian Championship and Commonwealth Championship (held 01 time in 04 years), SAF Games If he participates, will also be considered eligible. 1. The player will have to give an affidavit along with his application that he has never been a participant in the consumption of drugs in any competition/championship or sports event nor has he been found guilty in this case. 1. The applicant shall also submit an oath that he has not been found guilty in any suit by the Hon'ble Court nor has he been found guilty in connection with sexual harassment 1. The players who have the above eligibility will be given the Laxman Award to the male player and Rani Laxmi Bai Award to women players included in 31 sports (Swimming, Archery, Athletics, Badminton, Basketball, Boxing, Canoeing & Kayaking, Cycling, Horse Riding, Fencing, Football, Gymnastics, Handball, Hockey, Judo, Kabaddi, Kho - Kho, Rowing, Shooting, Table Tennis, Lan Tennis, Volleyball, Weightlifting/Best physics, Wrestling, Taekwondo, Yachting, Golf, Cricket, Soft Tennis, Karate and Wushu) by conferring Lakshman/Rani Lakshmi Bai Award to male/female sportsperson who bring laurels to the state and the country by achieving significant achievements in the respective sports. 1. One person will be rewarded only once. 1. The Government may cancel the name of any person from the list of awardee sportspersons and in that direction, such person shall be required to return the statue and inscription given to him. Beneficiary women must be above 18 years of age. 1. Benefits will be provided only to the first two deliveries of the worker.

If more than one girl child in first and second delivery, all girls are eligible. 1. Applies to legally adopted girl child (first girl only) in childless families. 1. Birth of girl child must be registered. 1. Girl must remain unmarried until 18 to receive fixed deposit. 1. Benefits will not be provided eligible if recipient of another similar government scheme.
schemes.benefits:

- Registered male workers: ₹6,000/- lump sum.
- Female workers for institutional delivery: 3 months' minimum wage equivalent + ₹1,000/- medical bonus.
- Female workers for miscarriage: 6 weeks' minimum wage equivalent.
- Female workers for sterilization: 2 weeks' minimum wage equivalent.
Shishu Hitlabh (Child benefits)
- For son: ₹20,000/- lump sum.
- For daughter: ₹25,000/- lump sum.
- For first or second daughter, or legally adopted daughter: ₹25,000/- fixed deposit (matures if unmarried at 18).
- For handicapped daughter: ₹50,000/- fixed deposit (matures if unmarried at 18).

schemes.name: Kanyashree Prakalpa
schemes.description: The scheme to improve the status and wellbeing of girls, specifically those from socio-economically disadvantaged families, through Conditional Cash Transfers by: Incentivising them to continue in education for a longer period of time, and complete secondary or higher secondary education.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a resident of West Bengal. 1. The applicant should be enrolled and regularly attending an educational institution. 1. The applicant should be unmarried. 1. The applicant should be from a family where the total income is not more than ₹1,20,000/- per year. 1. The applicant for the Annual Scholarship (K1) should be between the ages of 13 and 18 years. 1. The applicant for the One-Time Grant (K2) should be between the ages of 18 and 19 years. 1. The applicant should have a bank account opened in their own name, and the name on the application form must match the name on the bank account. 1. The educational institution that the applicant is studying in must be registered in West Bengal and recognized by the government.
> **Educational Institution Type Eligibility:****** The applicant will be eligible for Kanyashree's Annual Scholarship or the One-Time Grant if they are studying in any of the following: - In Class VIII or above in a school, madrassah, or equivalent open school course. - In a college or an equivalent open university course. - A Vocational Training Centre, Technical Training Centre, or Industrial Training Centre. - Girls enrolled in Sports Institutes may apply for the One-Time Grant; however, they cannot apply for the Annual Scholarship. > **Relaxations:** Girls who are specially-abled (with a disability of 40% or more), or are living in a Home registered under the Juvenile Justice Act, 2000, or have lost both parents, may apply for Kanyashree even if their family's income is more than ₹1,20,000/- per year.
schemes.benefits:
Kanyashree Prakalpa offers two types of benefits:
- Annual Scholarship (K1):
 A cash benefit of ₹1,000/- is paid annually to the girls in the age group 13 to 18 years (studying in Class VIII equivalent or above for every year that they remain in education, provided they are unmarried at the time.
- One-Time Grant (K2):
 A grant of ₹25,000/- is paid after a girl turns 18, provided that she was engaged in an academic or occupational pursuit and was unmarried.
schemes.application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Obtain the application form from the educational institution where the applicant is studying.
- For the Annual Scholarship, request the K1 form, which is printed on light green paper.
- For the One-Time Grant, request the K2 form, which is printed on light blue paper.
- If you have previously received a K1 and are still below 18 years and fulfilling other conditions (renewal), get in touch with your Head of Institution (HOI), who will verify your unmarried status and age and process your renewal.
- If you have received K1 and have crossed 18 years (up-gradation), collect a pre-printed K2 Up-gradation form from the institution, fill it up, and submit it. Do not fill up the colored K2 form if you have already received K1, as you will be treated as a duplicate applicant.
Step 2:
 
Open a bank account in your name :
- You must have a bank account in your name when applying for the Scheme. This is because Kanyashree's benefits will be sent by the government DIRECTLY to your bank account. If you do not have a bank account, ask your institution's head teacher to assist you in opening one.
- Please ensure the spelling of your name do not differ in the bank account and that of the application form.
Step 3:
 
Fill up the application form :
- You or your parents need to fill up all the details in the form. Again, your teachers and head teacher may be approached to help if you are not sure what to do. Your form needs to be signed by several people – check page 3 of the application form.
Caution:
- Check the spelling of your name that you are going to enter in the application form. It must be the same as that of your bank account.
- Please enter your mobile number correctly because you will get updates regarding the status of your application on your mobile number entered in the form.
- Put your signature on pages 1,2, and 3 of the form.
- Preserve the acknowledgement form for further tracking of your application.
Step 4:
 
Provide Necessary Documents:
- Submit photocopies of the required documents (refer to the "DOCUMENTS REQUIRED" section).
Step 5:
 
Collect Acknowledgement Receipt:
- The Kanyashree application form has a tear-off section on Page 3 titled "Acknowledgement as per WBRTPS Act 2013." When the completed application form is submitted to the Head Teacher, they will fill in the details, sign it, apply an official stamp, tear off that portion, and hand it over to the applicant. Keep this acknowledgement slip carefully as it contains the Form No., name, and other details, serving as proof of application for Kanyashree.
Track Your Application:
 Once the complete application with all supporting documents is handed over to the institution, the application is uploaded onto the 
Kanyashree Portal
. Applicants can track their application status on the portal using their Form No. (found on the Acknowledgement Slip). If the applicant changes institutions after receiving the annual scholarship, the previous Head of Institution must transfer the Kanyashree record online to the new institution to enable processing of further applications.

schemes.name: Pratyasha Scheme- West Bengal
schemes.description: The Government of West Bengal has undertaken a housing project scheme named ” PRATYASHA” for Police Personnel (from Inspectors to Constables) working in the West Bengal Police to materialize their dreams of owning houses.
schemes.eligibility:
1.	Any Police personnel in the ranks of Deputy Superintendent(Non-WBPS)/ Assistant Commissioner, Inspector, Sub-Inspector/ Sergeant, Assistant Sub-Inspector, Constable, and equivalent ranks of West Bengal Police and Kolkata Police can apply independently for dwelling units under this project as per their entitlement as mentioned below:
a)	Police personnel in the ranks of Deputy Superintendent(Non-WBPS)/ Assistant Commissioner, Inspector or Sub-Inspector/ Sergeant and equivalent ranks of West Bengal Police and Kolkata Police whose total pay (Basic Pay + Grade Pay) is ₹ 18,000/- (Rupees eighteen thousand) or more can apply for “A” category of dwelling units having flat area (approximate) 1123 sq. ft. per unit.
b)	Police personnel in the ranks of Inspector/ Sub-Inspector/ Sergeant or Assistant Sub-Inspector, Constable, and equivalent ranks of West Bengal Police and Kolkata Police whose total pay (Basic Pay + Grade Pay) is ₹15,000/- (Rupees fifteen thousand) or more can apply for “B” category of dwelling units having flat area (approximate) 926 sq. ft. per unit.
c)	Police personnel in the ranks of Assistant Sub-Inspector, Constable and equivalent ranks of West Bengal Police and Kolkata Police whose total pay (Basic Pay + Grade Pay) is ₹ 8,840/- (Rupees eight thousand eight hundred forty) or more can apply for “C” category of dwelling units having flat area (approximate) 794 sq. ft. per unit.
2.	One Police personnel in the above-noted ranks will be eligible to submit only one application for this Scheme (No other family member may apply separately).
3.	In the case of a joint application, only a member of the immediate family (spouse, parents, and children) is eligible to be the second applicant. The first applicant shall be the eligible personnel of West Bengal Police or Kolkata Police only.
4.	Only one flat will be allotted to the eligible applicant if he/she is selected through lottery on a random selection basis.
5.	The Corporation, however, reserves the right to relax any of these conditions whatsoever without assigning any reason.
<br>
schemes.benefits: 1. Construction of various types of flats for all Police Personnel of West Bengal.

https://www.kolkatapolice.gov.in/
). 
https://kolkatapolice.gov.in/images/PratyashaProject.pdf
Step 02: 
Application shall be accompanied by an A/C Payee Demand Draft/ Pay Order/Banker’s Cheque drawn in favour of the “Managing Director, West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited” on any Bank payable at Kolkata as per application money. Application with instruments payable outside Kolkata may be rejected at the discretion of WBPH & IDC Ltd. without citing any reason.
Step 03:
 The applicants should provide the details of his/ her Name, Father’s/ Husband’s Name, and Telephone No. on the reverse of the Demand Draft/ Pay Order/ Banker’s Cheque for application money.
Step 04: 
Application Form duly filled in and completed in all respect, duly signed by the applicant along with Demand Draft/ Pay Order/Banker’s Cheque for the amount as mentioned above and other documents of the General Terms & Conditions may be submitted at the office of West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited, Araksha Bhawan (3rd Floor), BlockDJ, Sector-II, Salt Lake City, Kolkata- 700 091.
Step 05:
 Applicants who wish to send their application by post, must send the application form along with application money as mentioned above, and other documents of General Terms & Conditions in the prescribed form, to the following address:- The Managing Director, West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited, Araksha Bhawan (3rd Floor), Block-DJ, Sector-II, Salt Lake City, Kolkata- 700091.

schemes.name: Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana
schemes.description: "Bina Mulya Samajik Suraksha Yojana (BM-SSY)" is a comprehensive social security scheme for unorganized workers in the state of West Bengal, India. It was launched by the West Bengal government in 2017.
schemes.eligibility:
1. Applicant  must be a resident of West Bengal.
1. Applicant  should be between 18-60 years of age.
1. Applicant family income should not exceed ₹ 6, 500/- per month except construction & transport workers. For construction & transport workers family income has no upper limit.
1. The period for calculation of average family income will be 12 months prior to the month in which application is made.
schemes.benefits:
- Provident Fund:  
The State Government will pay ₹ 55/- ( ₹ 25/- + ₹ 30/-) including the beneficiary and State Grant) per month. On retirement or death, the total amount along with interest is refunded.
- Health And Family Welfare:
- Medical Expenses
- Beneficiaries and their family members receive financial assistance for hospitalization and outdoor treatment of ailments covered under WBHS 2008.
- The maximum annual assistance is ₹ 20,000/-.
- Benefits include full coverage for clinical tests, medicine, and hospitalization.
- In case of hospitalization, the beneficiary receives compensation for loss of employment.
- Surgical Expenses
- Beneficiaries and their family members receive financial assistance for any kind of surgery.
- The maximum annual assistance is ₹ 60,000/-.
- Benefits include full coverage for clinical tests, medicine, and hospitalization.
- In case of hospitalization, the beneficiary receives compensation for loss of employment.
- Additional Provisions
- For accidents requiring hospitalization for five or more days, the beneficiary receives compensation for loss of employment.
- Claims for medical expenses, surgical expenses, and accident-related loss of employment are admissible at government or empaneled hospitals.
- Payment for loss of employment is applicable only to the registered beneficiary, not to family members.
- A financial year is considered a year for availing benefits.
- Death and Disability: 
 To provide social security to the nominees of the beneficiaries under the Yojana, the following benefit would be provided:-
- In cases of death of the beneficiary arising out of an accident : ₹ 2,00,000/- only
- In cases of normal death of the beneficiary : ₹ 50,000/- only : ₹ 50,000/-

schemes.name: Widow Pension-West Bengal
schemes.description: The West Bengal government's Women and Child Development and Social Welfare Department launched the "Widow Pension" scheme in 2010. This program aims to support widows facing economic hardship within the state.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The beneficiary must be widow girl/woman.
1. The beneficiary should attain the age of 60 years or more.
1. The beneficiary must be a citizen of India and a resident of West Bengal.
1. The beneficiary family income must not exceed ₹ 1,000/- per month.
1. The beneficiary must not be maintained by any of her relative.
1. The beneficiary must have been a resident of West Bengal for at least ten years on the date of making the application.
1. Professional beggars and mendicants are not eligible for the pension.
schemes.benefits: - Financials assistance of ₹1,000/- per Month through DBT mode to the beneficiaries.

schemes.name: Banglar Awaas Yojana (BAY)
schemes.description: "Banglar Awaas Yojana (BAY)" provides financial assistance to houseless households and those living in kutcha or dilapidated houses, helping them build pucca houses with essential amenities. The scheme offers financial assistance with additional support for toilets and unskilled workdays.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant must be an Indian citizen.
   1. The applicant must be enlisted in the Permanent Waiting List (PWL) based on SECC 2011 data.
   1. The applicant must be from a houseless household or living in a kutcha or dilapidated house.
schemes.benefits:
- Financial Assistance:
 Beneficiaries receive ₹1,30,000/- per house, credited in three installments (1st installment: ₹60,000/-; 2nd installment: ₹60,000/-; 3rd installment: ₹10,000/-).
- Wage Support:
 Beneficiaries are entitled to 95 unskilled mandays of wages under the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (MGNREGS).
- Assistance for Toilet Construction: 
Eligible beneficiaries enlisted in the BLS 2012 receive additional support for constructing toilets under "Mission Nirmal Bangla Prakalpa".
schemes.application_process:
Offline
Step 1:
 Visit the website of Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana-Gramin (PMAY-G) to check the 
Permanent Waiting List (PWL)
 which has been prepared by the Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD), GOI, based on SECC 2011 data.
Step 2: 
Select the concerned District, Block, Panchayat name and verify the captcha to check the list.
Step 3:
 Beneficiaries whose names are already enlisted in the PWL do not need to submit any additional application.
Step 4:
 The government sanctions houses every financial year based on the priority list and category-wise allocation of houses.
Step 5:
 If the applicant's name is included in the PWL, they will be automatically considered for assistance under the scheme.
Step 6:
 Eligible beneficiaries will be informed about the sanctioning process and receive financial assistance directly in their registered bank accounts in installments.

schemes.name: Banglar Yuba Sathi
schemes.description: The scheme aims to provide monthly financial assistance to educated unemployed youth of West Bengal to support them until they get employment or join another scheme. The objective is to ensure income support for eligible unemployed youth for a maximum period of 5 years.
schemes.eligibility: - The applicant must be a permanent resident of West Bengal. - The applicant must be aged between 21 and 40 years as on 01.04.2026. - The applicant must be unemployed. - The applicant must have passed the Madhyamik or equivalent examination. > **Notes:** - **Yearly verification of live status of the beneficiary of the scheme:** Once a year, the status of the beneficiary, whether alive or not will be verified. - **Stopping or withholding of financial assistance:** The Government may, after giving the beneficiary of the scheme an opportunity of being heard, stop or withhold financial assistance if: 1. The Financial Assistance has been found to be sanctioned on mistaken grounds. 1. The eligibility condition for the granting of financial assistance no longer exists.
schemes.benefits:
- Financial Assistance: 
₹1,500/- per month.
- Mode of Disbursement:
 Financial Assistance under the scheme will be credited by Direct Bank Transfer (DBT) to an AADHAR Linked Bank Account held solely by the eligible unemployed youth.
- Frequency of Disbursement:
 The benefit is disbursed monthly or at regular intervals.
- Validity of Benefit:
 The benefit is valid for a maximum period of 5 years, subject to continued eligibility or till employment or enrollment in any other scheme, whichever is earlier.

schemes.name: Bihar Student Credit Card Scheme
schemes.description: The Bihar Government launched the Bihar Student Credit Card (BSCC) scheme to provide financial aid to applicants to complete their higher education.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Bihar state and should be at least 25 years.
   1. The applicant must have passed the class 12th.
   1. The applicant must get admission to a course in an authorized institute.
   1. The applicant must complete the entire course.
schemes.benefits:
The eligible students will get the following benefits under this scheme:-
- A maximum loan amount of ₹4 lakh.
- Loan can be availed for polytechnic, technical and general courses.
- This assistance may be utilized for purchasing of books and laptops or pay any kind of fees.
- Repayment of the loan starts once the course has been completed and student have secured a job.
- The interest rate of the loan is 1% for Divyang, transgender, and girl students.
- The recovery procedures are flexible since the loan is government-owned.

https://www.7nishchay-yuvaupmission.bihar.gov.in/

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Shramshakti Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme aims to provide employment opportunities to men and women aged 18 to 45 from the minority community by offering vocational skill training in various fields or by providing loans through the Bihar State Minority Financial Corporation, Patna, for self-employment.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Bihar.
   1. The applicant should belong to a minority community.
   1. The applicant&#39;s age should be between 18 and 45 years.
   1. The applicant’s annual family income should be less than ₹4,50,000/-.
**Note 1:** Minimum educational qualification for most training courses is 8th/10th pass. The minimum educational qualification required for various training programs will be determined by the Bihar State Minorities Financial Corporation Limited, depending on the course syllabus.
**Note 2:** There is a 30% reservation for women and 3% reservation for disabled.
**Note 3:** If the reserved seats for women or disabled persons are not filled, they will be allocated to applicants from other minority categories.
schemes.benefits:
This scheme offers the following benefits:
- Self-Employment and Employment Opportunities:
 Men and women from minority communities will receive training to enhance their chances of self-employment and job opportunities.
- Easy Loans for Self-Employment:
 Candidates can avail loans at simple interest rates through the "Mukhyamantri Alpsankhyak Rozgar Rinn Yojana" after completing their training.
- Training Locations: 
Training will be provided by selected institutions at district headquarters, in locations chosen by these institutions.
- Free Study Materials and Tool Kits:
 Trainees will receive study materials and tool kits at no cost from the institutes.
- Certificates: 
Upon successful completion of the training, certificates will be awarded by the training institutions.
Note 1: 
Trainees are responsible for arranging their own accommodation and food. However, if the training institution provides residential facilities, the fees for accommodation and food, as fixed by institutions recognized by the State or Central Government, will be paid directly to the institution by the Bihar State Minorities Financial Corporation Limited from the scheme's funds.
Note 2:
 If residential facilities are not available at the training site, a stipend of ₹1500/- per month will be provided to trainees through the training institute during their training period.
schemes.application_process:
Online
Eligible candidates from minority communities interested in applying for various skill development training programs can submit their applications online via the Bihar State Minorities Financial Corporation Limited's website: 
https://bsmfc.org/
Registration Process:
Step 1: 
Visit the official website: 
https://bsmfc.org/
Step 2: 
On the homepage, click on the ‘Apply for Skill Training’ link.
Step 3: 
A new page will open; click on ‘Click Here to Apply’
Step 4: 
If you are a new user, click on ‘
New Registration
’.
Step 5: 
Complete the registration form with all required details and click on the ‘Register’ button.
Step 6: 
A confirmation message will appear. Note down the User ID and Password displayed on the screen.
Apply for Scheme:
Step 1:
 After registration, either continue on the same page or return to the website to 
login
.
Step 2:
 Enter your User ID and Password, then click on ‘Login’.
Step 3: 
Provide your personal details, educational qualifications, select the trade for training, and fill in the training details.
Step 4: 
Upload your photograph, signature, and other required documents, then submit the application.
Selection process:
- Shortlisting of applications received for various skill training programs from different districts of the state will be done by the Corporation.
- In case of receipt of more applications than the prescribed number of seats in the same training program in the same district, preference will be given to applicants with higher qualifications. In case of equal qualification, preference will be given to the applicant with older age.
- After shortlisting, the list and application form will be sent to the District Minority Welfare Office of the concerned district while allotting the district and training program to the shortlisted applicants.
- The final selection of the applicants will be done by the selection committee constituted under the chairmanship of the Deputy Development Commissioner of the concerned district.
Security Deposit:
- After final selection for the skill training programme (at the time of enrolment), the selected candidate will have to deposit ₹1,000/- as refundable security deposit, which will be returned to them after successful completion of the training programme.
User Manual

schemes.name: Financial Assistance for Marriage (BBOCWWB)

schemes.name: Krishi Input Anudan Yojana
schemes.description: The scheme aims to provide agricultural input subsidies in affected areas due to the rise in water levels of rivers like Ganga and Kosi and flood-related crop damage from heavy rainfall.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a farmer.
   1. The applicant should belong to the reported Panchayats of the identified blocks in the specified districts.
   1. The applicant must be registered on the DBT portal.
   1. Applicant&#39;s bank account must be linked with the Aadhaar number,
> Note: Farmers cultivating on others&#39; land will be identified and verified as &quot;actual cultivators&quot; through joint certification by the Agriculture Coordinator and Farmer Advisor. This process ensures that only genuine cultivators receive subsidy benefits.
schemes.benefits:
Subsidy Amounts per Hectare:
- Non-irrigated (year-dependent) crop areas: ₹8,500/-.
- Irrigated crop areas: ₹17,000/-.
- Perpetual/ perennial crops: ₹22,500/-.
Minimum Subsidy Amounts to Farmers:
- Non-irrigated (year-dependent) crop areas: ₹1,000/-.
- Irrigated crop areas: ₹2,000/-.
- Perpetual/ perennial crops: ₹2,500/-.

schemes.name: Bihar Shatabdi Asangathit Karyakshetra Kamagaar Evan Shilpakar Samajik Suraksha Yojana: Total Permanent Disability
schemes.description: “Total Permanent Disability” under the “Bihar Shatabdi Asangathit Karyakshetra Kamagaar Evan Shilpakar Samajik Suraksha Yojana” launched by the Labour Resources Department, Bihar, provides financial assistance of ₹75,000/- to the families of workers or craftsmen in case of permanent disablement.
schemes.eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Bihar state.
   1. The applicant must work as a worker or craftsman in the unorganized sector within Bihar.
   1. The applicant&#39;s age must be between 18 and 65 years.
> **Note****:** 
    - Applicant&amp;#39;s age will be calculated as of the 1st of July of any year.
    - **Total Permanent Disability:** Total permanent disability means that due to an accident, both the eyes or both hands or both legs of a worker or artisan become useless or one eye, one hand and one leg become useless. Apart from this, if both the eyes become useless due to paralysis or the brain stops working, then it will also be considered as permanent disability.
schemes.benefits:
- Financial Assistance:
 ₹75,000/-.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Bagwani Mission: Dragon Fruit Vikas Yojana
schemes.description: The scheme aims to expand dragon fruit cultivation in 21 districts of the state, providing financial assistance to ryot farmers for establishing and maintaining dragon fruit plantations.

schemes.name: Rythu Bandhu
schemes.description: Rythu Bandhu is Telangana's farm investment support scheme that helps land-owning farmers meet seasonal cultivation expenses.
schemes.eligibility: Resident land-owning farmers in Telangana with eligible agricultural land records can receive assistance.
schemes.benefits: Seasonal per-acre investment assistance is transferred directly to eligible farmers before each crop season.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiary records are identified from state land databases and assistance is credited through the state's agriculture delivery system.

schemes.name: Kalyana Lakshmi
schemes.description: Kalyana Lakshmi provides one-time marriage assistance to eligible women from economically weaker families in Telangana.
schemes.eligibility: Bride must be a Telangana resident from an eligible community and income category, with age and documentation requirements fulfilled.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial assistance is sanctioned to support marriage expenses.
schemes.application_process: Apply online through the Telangana ePASS platform and submit identity, income and marriage-related documents for verification.

schemes.name: Shaadi Mubarak
schemes.description: Shaadi Mubarak is a marriage assistance scheme for eligible minority women from low-income households in Telangana.
schemes.eligibility: Minority community applicants meeting age, income and residence conditions may apply before or after marriage within the permitted window.
schemes.benefits: Eligible beneficiaries receive a one-time financial grant for marriage support.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed online through Telangana ePASS with Aadhaar, bank, income and marriage documents.

schemes.name: KCR Kit
schemes.description: KCR Kit supports pregnant women delivering in government health institutions and encourages institutional deliveries.
schemes.eligibility: Pregnant women delivering in eligible government facilities in Telangana can avail the scheme subject to health department conditions.
schemes.benefits: The scheme provides mother-and-child care kits and cash support linked to antenatal care and institutional delivery milestones.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries are enrolled through government health facilities during pregnancy registration and delivery.

schemes.name: Kanti Velugu
schemes.description: Kanti Velugu is Telangana's mass eye-screening initiative for early detection and treatment of vision problems.
schemes.eligibility: Residents attending notified screening camps or facilities in Telangana can access services.
schemes.benefits: Free eye screening, spectacles and referrals for further treatment are provided.
schemes.application_process: Attend notified eye camps or health facilities and complete screening through the public health system.

schemes.name: Dalit Bandhu
schemes.description: Dalit Bandhu is an entrepreneurship support scheme that helps eligible Dalit families establish livelihood enterprises.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible Scheduled Caste beneficiary families identified under state guidelines can be selected by the district administration.
schemes.benefits: Capital support is provided for setting up income-generating enterprises chosen by the beneficiary family.
schemes.application_process: Selection is carried out by the state through district-level identification, enterprise planning and release of assistance.

schemes.name: Gruha Lakshmi Scheme
schemes.description: Gruha Lakshmi assists eligible women from weaker sections to construct a house on their own plot.
schemes.eligibility: Women beneficiaries from targeted communities with eligible house sites and state-approved documentation can apply.
schemes.benefits: The scheme provides financial assistance for house construction in installments.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through the state housing administration and local verification channels.

schemes.name: 2BHK Dignity Housing Scheme
schemes.description: The 2BHK Dignity Housing Scheme provides double-bedroom houses to eligible poor households in Telangana.
schemes.eligibility: Economically weaker households identified under state housing norms and local verification processes are considered.
schemes.benefits: Selected families receive a double-bedroom pucca house without direct beneficiary contribution.
schemes.application_process: Identification and allotment are carried out by the state government and local urban or rural housing authorities.

schemes.name: BC Bandhu
schemes.description: BC Bandhu supports backward class families with financial assistance for self-employment and small enterprise creation.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible backward class beneficiaries selected under state guidelines can receive assistance for approved livelihood activities.
schemes.benefits: The scheme offers capital support for self-employment ventures and economic upliftment.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries are identified through the backward classes welfare administration and district-level scrutiny.

schemes.name: Aarogyasri Health Care Scheme
schemes.description: Aarogyasri provides cashless tertiary medical care for eligible low-income families in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible Telangana families covered under the state's health assurance criteria may avail treatment in empanelled facilities.
schemes.benefits: Cashless treatment for listed procedures, hospitalization support and post-treatment follow-up are available.
schemes.application_process: Use the Aarogyasri health card or eligibility record at empanelled hospitals for authorization and treatment.

schemes.name: YSR Rythu Bharosa
schemes.description: YSR Rythu Bharosa supports farmers and tenant cultivators with annual investment assistance for cultivation.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible farmer and cultivator households recognized under Andhra Pradesh guidelines may receive support.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is credited in installments during the agricultural cycle.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiary identification and transfers are handled through village secretariats and the state beneficiary management system.

schemes.name: Amma Vodi
schemes.description: Amma Vodi supports mothers or guardians to encourage school education for children in eligible households.
schemes.eligibility: Mothers or guardians of school-going children meeting state income and schooling criteria can qualify.
schemes.benefits: Annual assistance is transferred to support schooling expenses.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries are identified through school education records and state welfare verification systems.

schemes.name: YSR Cheyutha
schemes.description: YSR Cheyutha helps women from targeted communities build sustainable livelihoods through phased financial support.
schemes.eligibility: Women beneficiaries from eligible communities and age groups identified by the state can participate.
schemes.benefits: Multi-year financial support is provided to strengthen livelihood activities and income generation.
schemes.application_process: Applications and verification are managed through village secretariats and the state welfare delivery platform.

schemes.name: YSR Aarogyasri
schemes.description: YSR Aarogyasri offers cashless health coverage for eligible families in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible low-income families recognized under the state's health assurance framework can use the scheme.
schemes.benefits: Cashless secondary and tertiary care treatment is available for notified procedures.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries use the scheme through empanelled hospitals and state health authorization systems.

schemes.name: Jagananna Vidya Deevena
schemes.description: Jagananna Vidya Deevena reimburses full tuition fees for eligible students pursuing higher education.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible students in approved higher education institutions who meet residence and income norms can benefit.
schemes.benefits: Tuition fee reimbursement is released in phases directly for continuation of studies.
schemes.application_process: Institutions and beneficiaries are verified through the state's higher education welfare systems.

schemes.name: Jagananna Vasathi Deevena
schemes.description: Jagananna Vasathi Deevena provides support for hostel and mess expenses to eligible higher education students.
schemes.eligibility: Students covered under the state's fee-support framework and meeting welfare criteria can receive assistance.
schemes.benefits: Financial support is credited for boarding and lodging expenses during studies.
schemes.application_process: Student eligibility is verified through institutional records and the state welfare portal.

schemes.name: YSR Asara
schemes.description: YSR Asara helps women self-help group members by easing the burden of certain outstanding loans.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible women SHG members identified under the state programme can be covered.
schemes.benefits: Phased financial support is provided to restore bank credit discipline and strengthen livelihoods.
schemes.application_process: Implementation is coordinated through the SERP and welfare delivery ecosystem.

schemes.name: YSR Kapu Nestham
schemes.description: YSR Kapu Nestham provides income support to eligible women from Kapu community households.
schemes.eligibility: Women beneficiaries from eligible Kapu, Balija, Ontari and Telaga communities meeting state criteria may apply.
schemes.benefits: Annual financial assistance is released in installments for livelihood support.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through the beneficiary management system and village secretariats.

schemes.name: Jagananna Thodu
schemes.description: Jagananna Thodu provides interest-free working capital support to small street vendors and traditional artisans.
schemes.eligibility: Identified street vendors and small urban informal workers in Andhra Pradesh can access the scheme.
schemes.benefits: Interest reimbursement and working capital support help sustain small businesses.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries are mapped through local bodies and assisted through the state welfare platform.

schemes.name: YSR Sunna Vaddi
schemes.description: YSR Sunna Vaddi reimburses interest burden for eligible women self-help group loans and other targeted beneficiaries.
schemes.eligibility: Women SHG members and other eligible categories notified by the state can receive support.
schemes.benefits: Interest reimbursement improves access to affordable credit and repayment discipline.
schemes.application_process: Eligible accounts are verified by the state welfare and banking support system before transfer.

schemes.name: Gruha Lakshmi
schemes.description: Gruha Lakshmi offers direct income support to the woman head of eligible households in Karnataka.
schemes.eligibility: Women heads of households classified under eligible ration card categories can benefit subject to state rules.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance is credited directly to the beneficiary's bank account.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted and verified through Seva Sindhu and linked departmental databases.

schemes.name: Gruha Jyothi
schemes.description: Gruha Jyothi provides free electricity up to the notified consumption slab for eligible domestic consumers.
schemes.eligibility: Domestic electricity consumers meeting the notified usage criteria can register for the benefit.
schemes.benefits: Households receive zero-bill support up to the approved free unit limit.
schemes.application_process: Consumers enroll through Seva Sindhu or notified service channels with electricity connection details.

schemes.name: Anna Bhagya
schemes.description: Anna Bhagya supplements food security support for eligible ration card holders in Karnataka.
schemes.eligibility: Priority and Antyodaya ration card households covered by the state's food security rules can receive the benefit.
schemes.benefits: Additional food grain entitlement or equivalent cash support is provided under the programme.
schemes.application_process: Eligible households are identified through the public distribution system database.

schemes.name: Shakti Scheme
schemes.description: The Shakti Scheme allows eligible women to travel free in notified state-run buses within Karnataka.
schemes.eligibility: Women residents and other eligible categories notified by the state can use the facility with valid identification.
schemes.benefits: Free travel in designated KSRTC services improves mobility and access to work, study and services.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries use the scheme through ticketing and identity verification on notified bus services.

schemes.name: Yuva Nidhi
schemes.description: Yuva Nidhi supports unemployed graduates and diploma holders with temporary financial assistance while they seek jobs.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible Karnataka graduates and diploma holders registered as unemployed under state rules may apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly assistance is provided for a limited period subject to continued eligibility.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed on Seva Sindhu with educational and unemployment registration details.

schemes.name: Vidyasiri Scholarship
schemes.description: Vidyasiri assists eligible students from disadvantaged groups with scholarship and hostel support for higher education.
schemes.eligibility: Students belonging to eligible communities and institutions in Karnataka can apply subject to income criteria.
schemes.benefits: The scheme provides scholarship, food and hostel support to continue studies.
schemes.application_process: Students apply through the State Scholarship Portal with required academic and income records.

schemes.name: Raitha Shakti Yojane
schemes.description: Raitha Shakti Yojane supports farmers with subsidies on certified seeds and key agricultural inputs.
schemes.eligibility: Registered Karnataka farmers procuring eligible inputs through approved channels can receive assistance.
schemes.benefits: Input subsidies reduce cultivation costs and encourage timely sowing.
schemes.application_process: Farmers register and purchase through the notified agriculture department and Raitha Samparka systems.

schemes.name: Arogya Karnataka
schemes.description: Arogya Karnataka provides health assurance and cashless treatment support through the state's public and empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Residents meeting state health scheme criteria can access services with valid identification.
schemes.benefits: Cashless treatment and referral support are available for listed procedures and hospital care.
schemes.application_process: Patients access the scheme through government or empanelled hospitals and the health authorization process.

schemes.name: Mathru Poorna
schemes.description: Mathru Poorna improves maternal nutrition by providing supplementary meals to pregnant and lactating women.
schemes.eligibility: Pregnant and lactating women registered through Anganwadi centres in eligible areas can receive benefits.
schemes.benefits: Nutritious meals and supplementary nutrition support improve maternal health outcomes.
schemes.application_process: Enrollment and service delivery happen through the Women and Child Development network and Anganwadi centres.

schemes.name: Ksheera Bhagya
schemes.description: Ksheera Bhagya provides milk to school children to improve nutrition and learning readiness.
schemes.eligibility: Children studying in eligible government and aided schools covered by the programme can benefit.
schemes.benefits: Milk is supplied on notified school days under the nutrition programme.
schemes.application_process: Implementation is handled through the school education system and milk federation supply chains.

schemes.name: Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thogai
schemes.description: Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thogai provides monthly income support to eligible women heads of households in Tamil Nadu.
schemes.eligibility: Women heads of family meeting state income, residence and ration-card conditions can receive assistance.
schemes.benefits: Monthly financial assistance is transferred directly to eligible beneficiaries.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through camps, e-sevai channels and departmental verification systems.

schemes.name: Pudhumai Penn Scheme
schemes.description: Pudhumai Penn encourages girls from government schools to pursue higher education with monthly support.
schemes.eligibility: Female students from eligible government schools admitted to higher education programmes can benefit.
schemes.benefits: Monthly assistance is credited to support continuation in college or professional courses.
schemes.application_process: Students register through the higher education process and state verification channels.

schemes.name: Naan Mudhalvan
schemes.description: Naan Mudhalvan is a skills and career guidance initiative that equips students and youth for higher studies and employment.
schemes.eligibility: Students and youth in Tamil Nadu institutions covered by the programme may participate.
schemes.benefits: Career guidance, skill courses, placement preparation and mentorship support are provided.
schemes.application_process: Candidates enroll through participating institutions and the state's programme platform.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme
schemes.description: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme provides morning meals to young students in government schools.
schemes.eligibility: Children enrolled in notified government primary schools are covered under the programme.
schemes.benefits: Nutritious breakfast helps improve attendance, concentration and nutrition outcomes.
schemes.application_process: Meals are served directly through participating government schools.

schemes.name: Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam
schemes.description: Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam delivers doorstep screening and follow-up care for selected non-communicable diseases.
schemes.eligibility: Residents in covered areas needing community-based health screening and support can access services.
schemes.benefits: Home-based screening, medicine delivery and referral support are provided.
schemes.application_process: Public health teams identify and serve beneficiaries through field visits and local health facilities.

schemes.name: Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
schemes.description: CMCHIS offers cashless health insurance support for eligible families in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Families meeting the state's prescribed income and enrollment conditions can use the scheme.
schemes.benefits: Cashless treatment for covered medical procedures is available through empanelled hospitals.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries enroll through designated channels and avail services using scheme identification at hospitals.

schemes.name: Illam Thedi Kalvi
schemes.description: Illam Thedi Kalvi supports foundational learning recovery for school students through community-based classes.
schemes.eligibility: Children in targeted localities covered by the programme can attend the learning sessions.
schemes.benefits: Supplementary after-school learning support improves reading, writing and numeracy skills.
schemes.application_process: Sessions are organized locally by volunteers and school education teams in covered habitations.

schemes.name: Amma Baby Care Kit Scheme
schemes.description: The Amma Baby Care Kit Scheme supports mothers delivering in government institutions with essential newborn care items.
schemes.eligibility: Women delivering in eligible government medical institutions can receive the baby care kit.
schemes.benefits: Newborn essentials and mother-care items are provided after delivery.
schemes.application_process: Distribution is handled through participating government hospitals and maternity facilities.

schemes.name: NEEDS Scheme
schemes.description: The New Entrepreneur-cum-Enterprise Development Scheme supports first-generation entrepreneurs with training and credit-linked subsidy.
schemes.eligibility: Educated first-generation entrepreneurs meeting age, education and project criteria can apply.
schemes.benefits: Entrepreneurship training, margin money support and bank-linked finance are facilitated.
schemes.application_process: Applicants submit project proposals through the industry department's notified application process.

schemes.name: Solar Powered Green House Scheme
schemes.description: Tamil Nadu's Green House Scheme supports vulnerable rural households with durable housing.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible rural families identified by the state and local bodies can be selected under housing guidelines.
schemes.benefits: Beneficiaries receive assistance for a pucca house with basic amenities and solar support features.
schemes.application_process: Selection and construction are handled through the rural development administration.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana
schemes.description: Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana provides monthly income support to eligible women in Maharashtra.
schemes.eligibility: Women beneficiaries meeting the state's age, income and family conditions can apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly direct benefit support is credited to the beneficiary's bank account.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed through state camps, online channels and local verification systems.

schemes.name: Jyotiba Phule Jan Arogya Yojana
schemes.description: Jyotiba Phule Jan Arogya Yojana provides cashless hospitalization support to eligible families in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible low-income and targeted category households in Maharashtra can access covered treatment.
schemes.benefits: Cashless treatment for listed procedures is available through network hospitals.
schemes.application_process: Patients avail the scheme through empanelled hospitals and the state authorization mechanism.

schemes.name: Majhi Kanya Bhagyashree Scheme
schemes.description: Majhi Kanya Bhagyashree encourages the welfare and education of girl children in eligible families.
schemes.eligibility: Families meeting the state's conditions for girl-child promotion and birth registration can apply.
schemes.benefits: The scheme provides financial security support linked to the girl child's age and education milestones.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through women and child development channels with required certificates.

schemes.name: Lek Ladki Yojana
schemes.description: Lek Ladki Yojana provides staged financial support for girls from eligible families in Maharashtra.
schemes.eligibility: Girls born in eligible ration card households and meeting state conditions can receive support.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is released at key milestones from birth to adulthood.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiary details are processed through women and child development systems and local authorities.

schemes.name: Balasaheb Thackeray Aapla Dawakhana
schemes.description: Aapla Dawakhana strengthens urban primary healthcare with easy access to consultation, diagnostics and medicines.
schemes.eligibility: Residents using notified urban primary care facilities can access the services.
schemes.benefits: Free or subsidized primary healthcare consultation, tests and medicines are provided.
schemes.application_process: Patients visit participating clinics and register for services at the point of care.

schemes.name: Shiv Bhojan Thali Yojana
schemes.description: Shiv Bhojan offers affordable cooked meals through designated centres across Maharashtra.
schemes.eligibility: Any resident or worker visiting notified Shiv Bhojan centres can purchase meals at the subsidized rate.
schemes.benefits: Nutritious meals are made available at an affordable price.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries visit approved Shiv Bhojan centres and obtain meals directly.

schemes.name: Chief Minister Employment Generation Programme
schemes.description: CMEGP supports new micro and small enterprises with margin money assistance and bank-linked finance.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible entrepreneurs, youth and groups planning a viable new enterprise in Maharashtra may apply.
schemes.benefits: The programme provides subsidy support linked to approved project cost and bank finance.
schemes.application_process: Applicants submit project proposals through the state's enterprise support channels for scrutiny and bank sanction.

schemes.name: Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Merit Scholarship
schemes.description: This scholarship supports meritorious students from eligible communities pursuing higher education.
schemes.eligibility: Students meeting community, academic performance and income criteria can apply through the state scholarship system.
schemes.benefits: Financial support helps cover study-related expenses in approved courses and institutions.
schemes.application_process: Students apply through the state scholarship portal with academic, caste and income documents.

schemes.name: Sharad Pawar Gram Samridhi Yojana
schemes.description: Sharad Pawar Gram Samridhi Yojana supports rural asset creation and allied livelihood activities in villages.
schemes.eligibility: Rural households and beneficiary groups identified under programme guidelines can participate.
schemes.benefits: Assistance is provided for creating productive rural assets and livelihood infrastructure.
schemes.application_process: Implementation is carried out through local self-government and rural development departments.

schemes.name: Mahajyoti Training and Coaching Scheme
schemes.description: Mahajyoti supports students with training, coaching and competitive exam preparation opportunities.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible students from notified beneficiary categories in Maharashtra can enroll for approved support modules.
schemes.benefits: Coaching support, study material and skill enhancement help students prepare for higher opportunities.
schemes.application_process: Candidates register through the implementing agency and attend approved training programmes.

schemes.name: Delhi Ladli Scheme
schemes.description: The Delhi Ladli Scheme promotes the education and welfare of girl children through staged financial support.
schemes.eligibility: Girl children born in Delhi to eligible resident families and studying in recognized schools can be covered.
schemes.benefits: Financial deposits are made at different schooling stages and can be claimed at maturity.
schemes.application_process: Applications are routed through schools, hospitals and the designated district administration process.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Tirth Yatra Yojana
schemes.description: Mukhyamantri Tirth Yatra helps senior citizens undertake pilgrimage journeys organized by the Delhi government.
schemes.eligibility: Senior citizens meeting the age and residence conditions laid down by the Delhi government may apply.
schemes.benefits: The scheme covers travel and selected logistics for approved pilgrimage circuits.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through district offices and notified online channels as per each batch schedule.

schemes.name: Jai Bhim Mukhyamantri Pratibha Vikas Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme supports coaching for eligible students preparing for competitive examinations.
schemes.eligibility: Students from eligible communities and income groups residing in Delhi can apply for approved coaching support.
schemes.benefits: The scheme funds coaching fee support for notified competitive examinations.
schemes.application_process: Applicants register through the implementing department and submit community, income and educational proof.

schemes.name: Delhi Family Benefit Scheme
schemes.description: The Family Benefit Scheme gives one-time support to households facing the death of a primary breadwinner.
schemes.eligibility: Low-income Delhi resident families meeting the scheme's conditions after the death of the earning member can apply.
schemes.benefits: A one-time ex-gratia financial assistance amount is released to the family.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through district administration or e-district service channels with death and income records.

schemes.name: Delhi Old Age Assistance Scheme
schemes.description: This scheme provides social assistance pension support to senior citizens residing in Delhi.
schemes.eligibility: Senior citizens fulfilling the prescribed age, residence and income conditions can apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension assistance supports basic living expenses.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through the social welfare department and e-district services.

schemes.name: Delhi Widow Pension Scheme
schemes.description: Delhi's widow pension scheme provides monthly financial support to eligible widowed women.
schemes.eligibility: Widowed women residing in Delhi and meeting the income and residence requirements can apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension support is credited to the beneficiary's account.
schemes.application_process: Apply through e-district or district welfare channels with required identity and status documents.

schemes.name: Delhi Disability Pension Scheme
schemes.description: The disability pension scheme supports eligible persons with benchmark disabilities residing in Delhi.
schemes.eligibility: Persons with eligible disability certificates and residence in Delhi can apply subject to income norms.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension support helps meet recurring living needs.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed through the welfare department and e-district services with medical certification.

schemes.name: Merit Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC/Minority Students
schemes.description: Delhi offers merit-based scholarship support to students from eligible communities studying in recognized institutions.
schemes.eligibility: Students meeting academic, community and income conditions can apply for assistance.
schemes.benefits: Scholarship support helps with tuition and education-related expenses.
schemes.application_process: Students apply through the concerned welfare department or online scholarship process.

schemes.name: Mission Buniyad
schemes.description: Mission Buniyad strengthens foundational literacy and numeracy for school students needing extra academic support.
schemes.eligibility: Students identified by the school education system for foundational learning support are covered.
schemes.benefits: The programme provides targeted remedial learning and academic reinforcement.
schemes.application_process: Schools implement the intervention through structured learning plans and assessments.

schemes.name: Free Bus Travel Scheme for Women
schemes.description: Delhi's free bus travel scheme improves safe and affordable mobility for women in notified bus services.
schemes.eligibility: Women and eligible transgender riders using notified public bus services in Delhi can avail the scheme.
schemes.benefits: Zero-fare travel reduces commuting cost for education, work and daily needs.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries use pink tickets or the prevailing ticketing process on notified bus services.

schemes.name: Vahli Dikri Yojana
schemes.description: Vahli Dikri promotes the welfare and education of girl children through staged financial assistance.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible families with girl children meeting state income and documentation conditions can apply.
schemes.benefits: Financial support is released in phases linked to education and adulthood milestones.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through the Women and Child Development system and digital citizen services.

schemes.name: Namo Lakshmi Yojana
schemes.description: Namo Lakshmi supports girls' school education in Gujarat with targeted financial assistance.
schemes.eligibility: Girl students enrolled in eligible classes and institutions under the state's conditions can benefit.
schemes.benefits: Direct assistance helps reduce dropout risk and encourages continued schooling.
schemes.application_process: Enrollment and verification are handled through the school education system and state citizen platforms.

schemes.name: Namo Saraswati Vigyan Sadhana Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme encourages students to pursue science education in higher secondary classes.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible students taking science streams in approved institutions may receive support.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance supports science education continuation and learning materials.
schemes.application_process: Students apply through the education department's notified system with school verification.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Amrutum Yojana
schemes.description: Mukhyamantri Amrutum provides cashless healthcare for eligible families in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible low-income and targeted category families in Gujarat can access the scheme.
schemes.benefits: Cashless hospitalization and treatment for covered conditions are provided.
schemes.application_process: Beneficiaries obtain or use the MA card and avail treatment through empanelled hospitals.

schemes.name: PMJAY-MA Yojana
schemes.description: PMJAY-MA integrates central and state health protection support for eligible Gujarat families.
schemes.eligibility: Families listed under PMJAY or eligible state categories can use the integrated health cover.
schemes.benefits: Cashless hospitalization for covered procedures is available through network hospitals.
schemes.application_process: Patients use their eligibility record at empanelled hospitals for pre-authorization and treatment.

schemes.name: Ganga Swarupa Arthik Sahay Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme supports widowed women with financial assistance for livelihood and social security.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible widowed women residents of Gujarat meeting the scheme's prescribed criteria can apply.
schemes.benefits: Financial support is provided to improve income security and household stability.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through the social justice or digital citizen service channels.

schemes.name: Kunvarbai nu Mameru Yojana
schemes.description: Kunvarbai nu Mameru supports the marriage of daughters from eligible low-income families.
schemes.eligibility: Families belonging to eligible communities and income groups can apply for assistance at marriage time.
schemes.benefits: One-time financial support is provided for marriage expenses.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed through social welfare or citizen service channels with community and income proof.

schemes.name: Manav Garima Yojana
schemes.description: Manav Garima helps eligible beneficiaries start self-employment activities through toolkits and assistance.
schemes.eligibility: Individuals from eligible communities and low-income households can apply for approved trades.
schemes.benefits: The scheme supports self-employment with tools and livelihood assistance.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed online through Gujarat citizen service platforms.

schemes.name: Shravan Tirthdarshan Yojana
schemes.description: Shravan Tirthdarshan helps senior citizens undertake pilgrimage journeys under the state's approved programme.
schemes.eligibility: Senior citizens meeting age and registration conditions laid down by Gujarat can apply.
schemes.benefits: Travel facilitation and related pilgrimage support are provided for approved circuits.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through the tourism or citizen service process when notified.

schemes.name: Ikhedut Farm Mechanisation Assistance
schemes.description: Gujarat supports farmers with assistance for farm mechanisation and equipment purchases under the Ikhedut platform.
schemes.eligibility: Registered farmers meeting scheme-specific landholding and equipment criteria can apply.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy support reduces the cost of approved agricultural machinery and implements.
schemes.application_process: Farmers apply online through the Ikhedut or Digital Gujarat service channels during open windows.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Health Insurance Scheme
schemes.description: Chiranjeevi provides cashless health insurance coverage for eligible families in Rajasthan.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible resident families enrolled under the state's health insurance framework can avail treatment.
schemes.benefits: Cashless hospitalization and treatment support are available in empanelled hospitals.
schemes.application_process: Families register through SSO or notified enrollment channels and use the scheme at empanelled hospitals.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Rajshri Yojana
schemes.description: Mukhyamantri Rajshri encourages the birth, immunization and education of girl children through phased benefits.
schemes.eligibility: Girl children born in Rajasthan and meeting institutional delivery and documentation norms can benefit.
schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is released at birth, vaccination and school education milestones.
schemes.application_process: Benefits are linked through health institutions, school enrollment and Jan Aadhaar or SSO verification.

schemes.name: Palanhar Yojana
schemes.description: Palanhar supports the upbringing and education of orphaned and vulnerable children in family care settings.
schemes.eligibility: Children under eligible vulnerable categories and their caretakers can apply under the state's rules.
schemes.benefits: Monthly assistance and education-related support are provided for child care.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted through the social justice system and verified by local authorities.

schemes.name: Kali Bai Bheel Medhavi Chhatra Scooty Yojana
schemes.description: This scheme rewards meritorious girl students with scooters to support continued higher education.

schemes.benefits: Scooter assistance improves mobility for college and higher studies.
schemes.application_process: Eligible students are selected through merit lists prepared by the education department.

schemes.name: Devnarayan Scooty Distribution Yojana
schemes.description: Devnarayan Scooty Distribution Yojana supports higher education for meritorious girl students from eligible communities.
schemes.eligibility: Girl students from notified beneficiary communities meeting merit conditions can be selected.
schemes.benefits: Scooter support helps students continue studies with improved mobility.
schemes.application_process: Applications and selection are handled through the state education and beneficiary verification process.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi Shahri Rozgar Guarantee Yojana
schemes.description: This urban employment guarantee scheme offers wage-employment opportunities in urban local bodies.

schemes.application_process: Applicants register with urban local bodies or SSO-linked systems and seek work demand entry.

schemes.name: Indira Rasoi Yojana
schemes.description: Indira Rasoi offers subsidized nutritious meals to the public through approved kitchen centres.
schemes.eligibility: Any resident or worker can visit a notified Indira Rasoi centre and purchase meals.
schemes.benefits: Affordable cooked meals improve food access for workers, migrants and low-income households.
schemes.application_process: Meals are accessed directly at approved centres on payment of the subsidized rate.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Kanyadan Yojana
schemes.description: Mukhyamantri Kanyadan supports the marriage of daughters from eligible low-income families in Rajasthan.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible families meeting community, residence and income conditions can apply for assistance.
schemes.benefits: One-time marriage support is released to help meet approved expenses.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted online or through welfare offices with income and marriage documents.

schemes.name: Tarbandi Yojana
schemes.description: Tarbandi Yojana supports farmers in fencing agricultural fields to protect crops from stray animals.
schemes.eligibility: Eligible farmers with agricultural land in Rajasthan can apply under notified conditions.
schemes.benefits: Subsidy support is provided for barbed-wire or approved fencing work.
schemes.application_process: Farmers apply through the agriculture department's notified scheme and verification channels.

schemes.name: Chief Minister Higher Education Scholarship Scheme
schemes.description: This scholarship supports meritorious students from low-income families pursuing higher education.

schemes.benefits: Scholarship assistance helps with college-level study expenses.
schemes.application_process: Applications are processed through the state's scholarship platform and institutional verification.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Kanya Sumangala Yojana
schemes.description: Kanya Sumangala supports the welfare and education of girl children through staged financial benefits.

schemes.benefits: Financial assistance is released in stages from birth to higher education milestones.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Samuhik Vivah Yojana

schemes.application_process: Applications are filed through district administration, urban local bodies or development blocks.

schemes.name: Nirashrit Mahila Pension Yojana

schemes.benefits: Monthly pension support is transferred directly to beneficiaries.
schemes.application_process: Applications are submitted online or through local offices with identity and bank details.

schemes.name: Old Age Pension Scheme
schemes.description: Uttar Pradesh's old age pension scheme supports senior citizens with regular financial assistance.
schemes.eligibility: Senior citizens meeting state age, income and residence criteria can apply.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension helps cover basic living expenses.
schemes.application_process: Applications are filed through the state social pension platform and local verification.

schemes.name: UP Scholarship Scheme

schemes.benefits: Scholarship assistance helps with fee and study-related expenses.
schemes.application_process: Students apply online through the scholarship portal and complete institute and district verification.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Abhyudaya Yojana
schemes.description: Abhyudaya offers free coaching and mentoring for competitive examinations to students in Uttar Pradesh.
schemes.eligibility: Students preparing for notified competitive exams and meeting program criteria can register.

schemes.name: Mukhyamantri Krishak Durghatna Kalyan Yojana

schemes.benefits: Compensation support is provided in cases of accidental death or notified disability.

schemes.name: Divyangjan Shadi Protsahan Yojana

schemes.name: Bal Seva Yojana

schemes.name: Rupashree Prakalpa

schemes.name: Aikyashree Scholarship
